name: Ewa Beach
tagline: A mellow neighborhood beach where local families set up camp all weekend — no tourists, no fuss.
parking: Street parking in the Ewa Beach neighborhood; free but limited
bestTime: Morning for calm water; weekdays for peace and quiet
talkStory: Ewa means 'crooked' or 'astray' in Hawaiian, referring to a surveying error in ancient land division. The actual beach park here is One'ula ('red sand'), named for the rusty coral-and-lava shoreline. This is a generational family beach — you will see the same canopies in the same spots every weekend. The water is shallow, warm, and calm enough for babies, but the real draw is the unfiltered local weekend vibe you will not find at any resort beach.
